channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: This money-saving emporium is like Facebook Marketplace for gift cards
item.description: You don’t have to be an avid reader of restaurant industry trade publications—though I can attest that they are oddly fascinating—to realize that everything’s getting more expensive. The good news is that there’s an easy way to counteract those rising menu prices.
item.dc:creator: Jared Newman, Fast Company

item.title: The latest DJI Osmo Action 6 image leak gives us our first look at the camera actually in action
item.description: Another picture of the DJI Osmo Action 6 has emerged, and it's likely that more leaks are going to follow.
item.dc:creator: David Nield, TechRadar

item.title: The truth about finding cheap airfare, from the head of Google Flights
item.description: Google Flights is one of the most popular flight aggregators on the web. The site lets users search millions of flights to find the best routes and prices that meet their needs. Unsurprisingly, millions of people use Google Flights to find the best deals on holiday tickets.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: Payroll scam hits US universities as phishing wave tricks staff
item.description: Microsoft reports Storm-2657 cybercriminals sent phishing emails to 6,000 addresses at 25 universities to steal payroll credentials and redirect funds.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The new MacBook Pro: Still a workhorse
item.description: Hello again, and thank you, as always, for spending time with Fast Company’s Plugged In. Apple is legendary for figuring out what people want before they realize they want it. But since 2021, its MacBook Pro hasn’t been like that at all.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: Trump administration pushes to get AI data centers on the grid faster
item.description: The Trump administration issued a proposal late Thursday that aims to help AI data centers get connected to the electric grid more quickly.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, The Hill

item.title: The hot issues for CIOs at TechCrunch Disrupt 2025
item.description: The TechCrunch Disrupt conference has long provided valuable insights to and about startups and their investors — but CIOs can learn by talking with startups too, and there’s plenty to learn about at this year’s event.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: In a California farming region, researchers are mapping rural heat to protect farmworkers
item.description: In the summers, the sky is jet black when Raul Cruz arrives at this Imperial Valley sugarcane field to start his day. He chops, cleans and bundles the crop, taking heed as the sun rises. It's hard work, but so is starting at 4 a.m.
item.dc:creator: DORANY PINEDA and JAE C. HONG, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: CIOs, be ready for agentic AI
item.description: CIOs are staring down a paradox. Employees are moving faster than ever with agentic AI. They’re using tools like Copilot, ChatGPT and embedded SaaS agents to build automations and streamline workflows on their own.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Python has a friend in Rust
item.description: Python 3.14 is here, and it’s bursting with goodies! Plus, Rust is making it easier to redistribute Python apps, PDM is simplifying Python package management, and Java is challenging Python for developing AI applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Hidden debug code returns from the dead as TP-Link routers face a wave of new critical root access flaws
item.description: TP-Link vulnerabilities reveal recurring coding issues and incomplete patches which could gift attackers full control of affected routers.
item.dc:creator: Efosa Udinmwen, TechRadar

item.title: AI is about to upend Google’s AdWords cash cow
item.description: Twenty-five years ago, Google unveiled Adwords, which pledged to enable advertisers “to quickly design a flexible program that best fits [their] online marketing goals and budget,” Google cofounder Larry Page said at the time. The principle was simple.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: American e-waste is causing a 'hidden tsunami' in Southeast Asia, report says
item.description: HANOI, Vietnam (AP) — Millions of tons of discarded electronics from the United States are being shipped overseas, much of it to developing countries in Southeast Asia unprepared to safely handle hazardous waste, according to a new report released Wednesday by an environmental watchdog.
item.dc:creator: ANIRUDDHA GHOSAL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: This is the world’s first vertical take-off AI-piloted fighter jet
item.description: San Diego-based Shield AI is developing a first of its kind fighter jet: a 2,000-mile-range pilotless plane that takes off and lands vertically and uses artificial intelligence to fly itself, even when adversaries jam navigation and communication systems.
item.dc:creator: Alex Pasternack, Fast Company

item.title: Reddit sues AI company Perplexity and others for 'industrial-scale' scraping of user comments
item.description: Social media platform Reddit sued the artificial intelligence company Perplexity AI and three other entities on Wednesday, alleging their involvement in an “industrial-scale, unlawful” economy to “scrape” the comments of millions of Reddit users for commercial gain.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Kevin O’Leary says ‘Marty Supreme’ blew millions by not using AI actors
item.description: The “Shark Tank” star said the production costs are outdated when AI can create realistic looking background characters.
item.dc:creator: Teddy Grant, NewsNation

item.title: OpenAI launches Atlas browser to compete with Google Chrome
item.description: OpenAI introduced its own web browser, Atlas, on Tuesday, putting the ChatGPT maker in direct competition with Google as more internet users rely on artificial intelligence to answer their questions.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: AI helps IT teams resolve incidents faster, report finds
item.description: New data suggests that generative artificial intelligence (genAI) is delivering measurable benefits for IT organizations and their IT Service Management (ITSM) efforts.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Ukraine unveils upgraded sea drone it says can strike anywhere in the Black Sea
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine's state security service has unveiled an upgraded sea drone it says can now operate anywhere in the Black Sea, carry heavier weapons and use artificial intelligence for targeting.
item.dc:creator: EFREM LUKATSKY and DEREK GATOPOULOS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Google and Apple face extra UK scrutiny over 'strategic' role in mobile platforms
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Britain’s antitrust watchdog on Wednesday targeted Google and Apple for their “strategic” roles in mobile ecosystems, opening the door for regulators to impose changes to their business practices to improve competition.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: AI’s dark side shows in Gartner’s top predictions for IT orgs
item.description: While AI certainly has the potential to change enterprise operations for the better, it also can present a few “insidious” challenges for IT leaders, according to analysts at the Gartner IT Symposium/Xpo going on this week in Orlando, Fla.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Intel’s Panther Lake chip could allow Windows 11 to morph into an agentic OS
item.description: Intel’s upcoming “Panther Lake” processor for AI PCs could be a key building block in the transition of Windows 11 into an AI agent-driven OS, analysts said this week. Panther Lake will be available in PCs starting early in 2026.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: AI gold rush sparks backlash against Core Scientific acquisition
item.description: CoreWeave’s $9 billion bid for Core Scientific undervalues the data center operator, one of its largest shareholders said Tuesday. It supported the recommendation of investment advisor Institutional Shareholder Services to reject the bid.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: With federal HR in flux, smaller teams are finding smarter ways to do more with less
item.description: "When you do any type of workforce reductions, you have to realize that you're also going to have attrition. Employees are going to leave," Rick Hammell said. The post With federal HR in flux, smaller teams are finding smarter ways to do more with less first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gerton, Federal News Network

item.title: AWS DNS error hits DynamoDB, causing problems for multiple services and customers
item.description: Monday got off to a bad start for Amazon Web Services users served by the company’s US-EAST-1 region, when a DNS problem rendered the DynamoDB API unreliable, with consequences for many AWS services and customers.
item.dc:creator: Info World
